story: A mushroom grower named Tomas had been cultivating oyster mushrooms for six years and used the same substrate formula every time. His supplier sent a new batch of hardwood sawdust that contained a slightly higher moisture content. Tomas dismissed this as unimportant and prepared the substrate exactly as he always had. His yields dropped significantly. He told a fellow grower, 'It's just the season. Summer harvests are always weaker.' He didn't test the moisture content or adjust his recipe, continuing to use the new sawdust without modification.
